avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of weakly bonded windslab will persist with further deposits continuing to develop with the strong winds. Unstable accumulations in wind sheltered terrain mainly above 850m particularly in sheltered gullies and on steep slopes with a NW to NE aspect. Fragile cornices will also exist above similar areas. Cross loading may affect some other wind sheltered aspects. Wind exposed slopes will be firm and often icy with better stability. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Staying dry, the freezing level will be around 400 metres for most of the period but will rise to around 700 metres by the end of the day. The strong winds will remain South-Easterly but start to ease in the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Last night the winds became South-Easterly and strengthened to near gale force these continued through today and were particularly strong over the higher summits. It has remained quite cold, the freezing level was around 400 metres and it was a dry day but with a high windchill element.
ObservedSnowStability: Wind redistribution is rapidly forming new areas of poorly bonded windslab in wind sheltered terrain. Most affected locations are wind sheltered gullies, steep slopes and coire rims with a North-West through North to North-East aspect above 850 metres. Fragile cornices exist above similar aspects. Avalanche activity noted today. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Additional weak windslab expected on all steep, sheltered West through North to North-East aspects above 900m. Buried weaknesses will persist on selected North-East and East aspects above 950m. Weak recently formed cornices above many of these places. Much better stability on ridges, and all recently wind scoured slopes and gullies where hard or crusty conditions will be found.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A trace of snowfall is forecast. Very strong South-East winds easing to strong South-South-Easterly later. Cold overnight though easing a little by late afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry and cold with very strong South-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Redistribution of snow led to new weak windslab accumulations in wind-sheltered locations, mainly very steep West through North to North-East aspects above 850m. Pockets of unstable snow also persist on selected very sheltered East aspects above 900m. New cornice development noted above some recent steep lee slopes. Good stability on heavily scoured windward aspects where hard and icy conditions prevail.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Poorly bonded windslab will continue to build with avalanches likely on steep slopes above 800 metres on West through North to North-East aspects.  South-West and East aspects will be affected by cross loading. Below. 800 metres and above 500 metres, unstable windslab will be present on steep banks and burn lines.  On these aspects cornices will be unstable.  Elsewhere the snowpack will become wind eroded and firm. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Storm force South-South-East winds will ease through the day to become strong to gale force. Some very light snowfall expected overnight and later in the day.  The 600 metre freezing level will rise to 700 then later 800 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with storm force South-South-East winds creating heavy drifting. The freezing level was around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: New accumulations of poorly bonded windslab have developed; this is most widespread above 800 metres on North-West to North-East aspects, cross loading has affected West and East aspects. On steep banks and burn lines unstable windslab is developing below 800 metres. Avalanche activity noted on a North aspect at 530 metres. On these aspects cornices are fragile. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snow will continue to be re-distributed. This will create isolated areas of unstable windslab, in wind sheltered locations, above 800 metres on steep West to Northerly aspects. Corrie rims and gully tops will be most affected, and fragile cornices will develop on these aspects. Existing instabilities on North-East to South-East aspects, above 800 metres will also persist within the recently deposited snowpack. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be cloudy with some patchy light snow throughout the period. Snow amounts are forecast to be minimal. Overnight Gale force East-South-Easterly winds will moderate through the day and veer to Strong Southerlies.  Freezing level will rise slowly to around 700 metres by Thursday afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry day with no precipitation. The winds were Strong East-South-Easterly with the  freezing level around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab persists in steep, wind sheltered locations above 800 metres, on mainly North-East to South-East aspects. Wind re-distribution of existing snow is also creating isolated areas of fresh windslab on West to North aspects, again, mainly above 800 metres in wind sheltered locations. Where scoured, the snow is firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable areas of windslab will remain above 900 metres in locations sheltered from the strong winds of late. Most affected will be West through North to North-East aspects. Below 900 metres there will be some consolidation. On windward aspects and locations exposed to the strong winds the snowpack will be scoured and generally stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A generally dry day with the gale force South-Easterly winds easing slightly. The freezing level will be around 600 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight and through the day strong winds blew from the South-South-East. It was generally dry with a freezing level of around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Although there was no fresh snowfall, there were large quantities of snow being re-distributed. Unstable deposits of windslab were being deposited on sheltered locations on West through North to North-East aspects above 800 metres. A substantial natural avalanche occurred on a North-West aspect at about 800 metres in the early hours of the morning. However, areas exposed to the strong winds tend to be scoured and stable. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab will be present in wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 900 metres, with moderately bonded snow present below this altitude. Fragile cornices will exist above these slopes. Cross-loaded accumulations may be also encountered on South-West and North-East aspects above 900 metres. Open slopes will be wind scoured and icy.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mainly dry with some light snow forecast from mid-afternoon. South-South-Easterly winds throughout the period, gale force overnight, strong during the day. 450 metre freezing level overnight, 550 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Severe gale to storm force South-Easterly winds. Occasional light snow showers. Freezing level around 450 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Persistent redistribution of snow has formed moderately to weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations only, on mainly West to North aspects above 900 metres. Moderately bonded accumulations are present below this altitude and cross-loaded deposits exist on South-West and North-East aspects above 900 metres. Fragile cornices have formed.  Most open slopes are wind scoured and icy, with exposed ridges stripped bare.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
